video.title: Origin -- 'Wide Eyed Angel' (Roberto Cano Remix)
video.description:

master.notes: Paranoid is Black Sabbath's second studio album, released September 1970 in the UK. The USA release was delayed until January 1971. This was the band's only album to top the UK Albums Chart until the release of 13 in 2013. Paranoid features several signature songs, including "War Pigs," "Iron Man," "Fairies Wear Boots," and the title track, which was the band's only Top 20 hit, reaching number 4 in the UK charts. The album has been the focus of many accolades. Rolling Stone, in 2017, called Paranoid the greatest metal album of all time.

master.notes:
Described in the liner notes as Ludovic Navarre's "quintessential works on [l=Fnac Music] and [l=F Communications] from '92 to '96" and the first time his "entire early works have been available since the demise of [l=Fnac Music Dance Division] in '94".
Issued as a single CD (plus an equivalent version on vinyl), a twin-disc set with the Boulevard compilation ([r=345970]) and a 2CD version, with and without a slipcase, subtitled The Complete Series For Connoisseurs. The latter was reissued digitally in 2012.
The Black Man contains uncredited samples of The Devil Jumped The Black Man' performed by Lightnin' Hopkins.
Alabama Blues contains uncredited samples of Stranger Here performed by Lightnin' Hopkins.

master.notes: Ultimately two years in the making, the 'Statement 1961' compilation was intended to celebrate the relaunch of the ironflame.de website, accompanied by the festival of the same name in Berlin. The exclusive contributions from the bands on DAT, CDr, etc. were sent as a mastering job to [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/16952326-Bypass-26]Bypass[/url] ([a597993] of [a104535]) @ [l70315], who converted all the contributions to MP3 on his personal computer and then disappeared. It took an enormous amount of time, energy, money, and ultimately unorthodox methods to retrieve all the original tapes. When the, elsewhere mastered, finished compilation was finally released as a super deluxe edition in June 2004, Robert Kuhn's bandmate at Rectal Surgery, [a574776] ([l13617] Records), uploaded all the unmastered MP3s that Robert had supplied him with to the then very popular illegal file-sharing portals Audio Galaxy, Napster, and Open Nap, thereby dealing a fatal financial blow to the ‘Statement 1961’ venture (33 exclusive unreleased tracks on a Hi-End product) and the young Ironflame label.
master.title: Statement 1961
